attacking_style: Does not rely on single explosive attacks; instead applies sustained climbing pressure through multiple accelerations, particularly on categorized climbs. Attacks often neutralized by opponents sitting on their wheel.
team_tactics: Aggressive tempo control using domestiques (DE PLUS, DEPLUS, Turner) to eliminate opponents through attrition on climbs and uncategorized drags. Deploys multi-rider trains to isolate rivals. Sometimes burns domestiques questionably early despite controlling race.
terrain_preference: Sustained climbs where tempo grinding can eliminate rivals, uncategorized technical sections before finals, crosswinds on descents for splitting groups. Exposed when opponents can sit on their wheel and counter.
vulnerability: Struggles when missing breakaway formations in Grand Tours with limited stage opportunities. Tempo-setting strategy vulnerable to wheel-sitting opponents who can neutralize repeated attacks. Questionable energy management burning domestiques too early.
key_patterns: Uses domestiques to set infernal tempo on climbs/drags pre-20km to eliminate weaker rivals (3 instances)
Deploys multi-rider trains to isolate and force solo chasing by rivals (2 instances)
Attacks neutralized by opponents sitting wheel rather than being distanced by power (2 instances)
Shows desperation chasing when missing breaks, especially late in Grand Tours (1 instance but pattern aligns)
Exploits technical terrain: crosswinds, descents, uncategorized drags for selection before organized finals (2 instances)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed desperation for stage wins in Vuelta, but limited recent data. 2022 displayed strong domestique depth but tactical execution mixed with questionable energy management decisions.
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 16
